channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Find the perfect free Zoom background at these 3 sites
item.description: Maybe I should begin this article by arguing that nothing spices up a mundane meeting like a creative, beautiful, or hilarious background for your Zoom calls. But the reality is that most of us just need to hide messy offices, guestroom beds, or dirty-dish-filled kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: La mitad de los líderes de la alta dirección abandonarán sus empresas en dos años
item.description: Una nueva encuesta de Gartner realizada a más de 200 directivos revela que el 56% de estos ve “extremadamente probable” renunciar a su trabajo actual en los próximos dos años, y el 27% planea hacerlo en los próximos seis meses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: High-stakes AI summit in Paris: World leaders, tech titans and challenging diplomatic talks
item.description: Major world political leaders are meeting for an AI summit in Paris, where challenging diplomatic talks are expected while tech titans fight for dominance in the fast-moving technology industry.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This new open source collaboration tool makes working with your colleagues even easier
item.description: Nextcloud Talk platform prioritizes data protection, offering secure communication, AI features, and real-time document collaboration.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Meta’s Ray-Ban smart glasses are having their Super Bowl moment
item.description: It’s game time for Meta’s wearables: The tech giant has bought two ad spots for its Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses during Sunday’s Super Bowl broadcast, including one that has Chris Hemsworth and Chris Pratt wreaking havoc on Kris Jenner’s art collection.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Visual Studio Code unveils Copilot code editing suggestions
item.description: A just-released update of Visual Studio Code, version 1.97, introduces a preview of Copilot Next Edit Suggestions, an AI-powered feature that predicts the next edit the user wants to make.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data
item.description: Democrats are turning to inspector general's offices, including those swept up in Trump's firing of several IG's last month, to investigate DOGE's data access. The post Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple could soon be forced to give away all your encrypted data to the UK government
item.description: The UK issued the order under the controversial Investigatory Powers Act. Now, experts fear Meta and Google could be the next target, warning against mass surveillance.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Beware of fake Reddit solutions delivering dangerous malware
item.description: Bad actors are now mimicking Reddit to spread malware that can steal personal information. CyberGuy shares what you need to know about fake Reddit pages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why I want glasses that are always listening
item.description: The trouble with virtual assistants is that they’re just so darn needy. Specifically, they need to be told exactly what to do. They sit around doing nothing until explicitly directed. They don’t take any initiative. They’re, for lack of a better word, lazy.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI chatbot war breaks out with DeepSeek debut, and the winner is…you
item.description: Less than a week after Chinese start-up DeepSeek rocked the tech industry, leading generative AI (genAI) model providers are scrambling to shore up their market foothold and find ways to emulate the newcomer’s tech efficiencies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Databricks acquires BladeBridge to aid data warehouse migrations
item.description: Data lakehouse provider Databricks has acquired data platform modernization software provider BladeBridge for an undisclosed sum to help its new customers move from rival data warehouses, such as Teradata, Snowflake, Amazon Redshift, and Microsoft SQL Server, to its lakehouse built atop Databricks S
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to easily transfer files and photos off a computer to your hard drive
item.description: It's not just about freeing up space on your computer, Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says. It's about protecting your digital memories and important documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
